LAW AND MOTION, 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT ANDREAS PAPALIOLIOS' SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR LIBEL, MR. PAPALIOLIOS SATISFIED HIS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT NON-INCIDENTAL ALLEGATIONS OF THE CLAIM ARISE OUT OF ACTIVITY PROTECTED BY THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE, BUT PLAINTIFFS ALSO SATISFIED THEIR BURDEN OF PROVIDING EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT THERE IS REQUISITE MINIMAL MERIT TO THIS CLAIM. AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION, MR. PAPALIOLIOS DID NOT SATISFY HIS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT NON-INCIDENTAL ALLEGATIONS OF EITHER OF THESE CLAIMS ARISE OUT OF ACTIVITY PROTECTED BY THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR #5976

AND CHRISTOPHER BENTLY'S MOTION TO SEAL CROSS-COMPLAINT OF ANDREAS PAPALIOLIOS AND ANY FUTURE FILINGS DISCLOSING ATTORNEY-CLIENT COMMUNICATIONS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO SEAL THE ENTIRE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS NOT NARROWLY TAILORED AND FAILS TO COMPLY WITH CRC 2.550(D)(4). THE MOTION DOES NOT EVEN HIGHLIGHT THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT OR PARKER COMPLAINT THAT PURPORTEDLY IMPLICATE THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN OPPOS TO DEMURRER

LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING RE: CROSS DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER BENTLY'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. THE REQUESTS FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE GRANTED. CROSS-DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER BENTLY'S ("MR. BENTLY") DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND FOR FAILURE TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. (CCP 430.10(E).) THE FACTS PLED IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT DO NOT SUFFICIENTLY PLEAD A BREACH OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT DOES NOT SUFFICIENTLY PLEAD THAT MR. BENTLY VIOLATED ANY STAY-AWAY ORDER; FOR EXAMPLE, IT DOES NOT PLEAD THAT MR. BENTLY WAS EVER WITHIN 50 YARDS OF CROSS-COMPLAINANT ANDREAS PAPALIOLIOS ("MR. PAPALIOLIOS"). AS TO THE PURPORTED FAILURE TO WITHDRAW REPORTS MADE TO CREDIT AGENCIES, THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT DID NOT OBLIGATE MR. BENTLY TO DO SO, AND THEREFORE, THERE IS NO ALLEGED BREACH BASED ON THE FACTS PLED. AS TO THE ALLEGED DISPARAGEMENT, THE CROSS-COMPLAINT DOES NOT ESTABLISH THAT MR. BENTLY ENGAGED IN ANY OF THE ACTS PLEADED. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT MR. PAPALIOLIOS WAS ASKED TO LEAVE THE BENTLY RESERVE BUILDING BY AN EMPLOYEE OF BENTLY RESERVE. BENTLY RESERVE WAS NOT A PARTY TO THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT, AND THERE APPEAR TO BE NO ALTER EGO OR AGENCY ALLEGATIONS IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER BENTLY'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT IS DENIED AS MOOT, IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON DEMURRER.(PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS BENTLY NOB HILL, L.P., AND CHRISTOPHER BENTLY'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRERS TO THE 1ST AND 5TH DEFENSES ARE SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COMPLAINT STATES A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR LIBEL INCLUDING THE NECESSARY ALLEGATIONS. THE DEMURRERS TO THE 3RD AND 7TH DEFENSES ARE OVERRULED. IT IS FOR THE TRIER OF FACT TO DETERMINE THE VIABILITY OF THIS DEFENSE AS TO THE VARIOUS ALLEGATIONS. THE COURT OF APPEAL DID NOT MAKE ANY BINDING FINDINGS THAT THE DEFENSE FAILS AS A MATTER OF LAW AS TO ALL ALLEGED LIBELS. THE DEMURRERS TO THE 4TH, 6TH, 10TH, AND 13TH DEFENSES ARE OVERRULED BECAUSE THEY ARE LEGAL DEFENSES BASED ON THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE COMPLAINT OR ADEQUATELY STATE THE NECESSARY FACTS. THE DEMURRERS TO THE REMAINING DEFENSES ARE SUSTAINED WITH 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE DEFENSES ARE TOTALLY CONCLUSORY AND DO NOT MEET THE PLEADING REQUIREMENTS OF FPI DEV. INC. V. NAKASHIMA, (1991) 231 CAL.APP.3D 367, 384. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

(CRC 2.550(d).) No opposition filed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sara Lerschen CSR6213.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MANUEL MARTINEZ, ESQ., 415.981.0550, FOR CROSS DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER BENTLY.
NITOJ PAUL SINGH, ESQ., 415.433.1700, FOR CROSS COMPLAINANT ANDREAS PAPALIOLIOS.

RULING: Argued and the Court MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING RE: CROSS DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER BENTLY'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. The requests for judicial notice are granted. Cross-Defendant Christopher Bently's ("Mr. Bently") demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend for failure to state a cause of action. (CCP 430.10(e).) The facts pled in the Cross-Complaint do not sufficiently plead a breach of the Settlement Agreement. The Cross-Complaint does not sufficiently plead that Mr. Bently violated any stay-away order; for example, it does not plead that Mr. Bently was ever within 50 yards of Cross-Complainant Andreas Papaliolios ("Mr. Papaliolios"). As to the purported failure to withdraw reports made to credit agencies, the Settlement Agreement did not obligate Mr. Bently to do so, and therefore, there is no alleged breach based on the facts pled. As to the alleged disparagement, the Cross-Complaint does not establish that Mr. Bently engaged in any of the acts pleaded. The Cross-Complaint alleges that Mr. Papaliolios was asked to leave the Bently Reserve Building by an employee of Bently Reserve. Bently Reserve was not a party to the settlement agreement, and there appear to be no alter ego or agency allegations in the Cross-Complaint. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MANUEL MARTINEZ, ESQ., 415.981.0550, FOR CROSS DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER BENTLY.
NITOJ PAUL SINGH, ESQ., 415.433.1700, FOR CROSS COMPLAINANT ANDREAS PAPALIOLIOS.

Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Plaintiffs Bently Nob Hill, L.P., And Christopher Bently'S Demurrer To Amended Answer To Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: MICHAEL DONNER, ESQ., 415.981.0550, FOR PLAINTIFFS BENTLY NOB HILL, L.P. AND CHRISTOPHER BENTLY.
NITOJ SINGH, ESQ., 415.433.1700, FOR DEFENDANT ANDREAS PAPALIOLIOS.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling RE: Plaintiffs Bently Nob Hill, L.P., And Christopher Bently'S Demurrer To Amended Answer To Complaint. The demurrers to the 1st and 5th defenses are sustained without leave to amend. The complaint states a cause of action for libel including the necessary allegations. The demurrers to the 3rd and 7th defenses are overruled. It is for the trier of fact to determine the viability of this defense as to the various allegations. The Court of Appeal did not make any binding findings that the defense fails as a matter of law as to all alleged libels. The demurrers to the 4th, 6th, 10th, and 13th defenses are overruled because they are legal defenses based on the allegations of the complaint or adequately state the necessary facts. The demurrers to the remaining defenses are sustained with 30 days leave to amend. The defenses are totally conclusory and do not meet the pleading requirements of FPI Dev. Inc. v. Nakashima, (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 384. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Notice Ofmotion And Motion To Seal X-Complaint Adn Any Future Filings Disclosing Attorney-Client Communications
Off Calendar Nov-07-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MANUEL A. MARTINEZ OF STEIN & LUBIN LLP, TEL. NO. 955-5025 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. HARMEET K. DHILLON OF DHILLON & SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 433-1700 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS BENTLY RESERVE, L.P. AND CHRISTOPHER BENTLY'S Motion To Seal Cross-Complaint of Andreas Papaliolios And Any Future Filings Disclosing Attorney-Client Communications is denied. Plaintiff's motion to seal the entire cross-complaint is not narrowly tailored and fails to comply with CRC 2.550(d)(4). The motion does not even highlight the allegations in the cross-complaint or Parker complaint that purportedly implicate the attorney-client privilege. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)

Ntc Of Special Mtn And Special Mtn To Strike By Deft Andreas G. Papaliolios
Off Calendar Jul-17-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Harmeet K. Dhillon, Esq. of Dhillon & Smith LLP, Tel. 415.433.1700, appearing for defendant and moving party;
Michael F. Donner, Esq., Tel. 415.981.0550, and Daniel K. Slaughter, Esq., 415.955.5037, of Stein & Lubin LLP, appearing for plaintiffs and opposing party.

The matter was argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ANDREAS PAPALIOLIOS' Special Motion To Strike is Denied in its entirety. As to the first cause of action for libel, Mr. Papaliolios satisfied his burden of showing that non-incidental allegations of the claim arise out of activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute, but plaintiffs also satisfied their burden of providing evidence showing that there is requisite minimal merit to this claim. As to the second and third causes of action, Mr. Papaliolios did not satisfy his burden of showing that non-incidental allegations of either of these claims arise out of activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR #5976
